name: Kawela Bay
tagline: A secluded crescent bay past Turtle Bay Resort that feels like you stepped into a postcard from old Hawaii.
parking: Park at the public access lot near Turtle Bay Resort; 10-minute walk along the shoreline trail
bestTime: Mornings for calm water; summer for swimming, winter for dramatic scenery
talkStory: Kawela Bay was a major filming location for LOST (the survivors' beach camp), Pirates of the Caribbean 4, and The Hunger Games: Catching Fire. The bay was part of the historic Marconi wireless telegraph station site in the early 1900s. A ten-minute walk past Turtle Bay Resort along a coastal ironwood trail rewards you with a crescent of sand you will likely have to yourself. The reef-protected water stays calm year-round, making it one of the few North Shore beaches safe for swimming even in winter.
